title: hypericum humifusum subsp. australe
intro: Hypericum humifusum subsp. australe, also known as low St. John's wort, is a perennial plant species within the St. John's Wort genus, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and low-growing habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of hypericin and hyperforin compounds. The health benefits of Hypericum humifusum subsp. australe include its ability to reduce anxiety and depression, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use is not recommended without consulting a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
